policy: Guarded checks may refuse to run unless their opt-in env var is set during ordinary development, but release-candidate readiness must have fresh successful results for every check listed here. A skipped-by-policy/error result is acceptable only before the mandatory milestone.

items.skipPolicyBeforeMandatory: May be skipped during ordinary development because Playwright global setup can restart/clean local E2E stack state.
May be skipped during ordinary development because it mutates local Anvil chain height by mining a block burst and depends on a running local E2E stack/indexer.
May be skipped during ordinary development because Playwright global setup restarts/cleans local E2E stack state and the journeys drive the full Docker stack.
May be skipped during ordinary development because it wipes local chain, IPFS, and Ponder data.
May be skipped during ordinary development because it restarts local services.
May be skipped during ordinary development because it probes public testnet DNS/TLS.
May be skipped during ordinary development because it probes public testnet HTTP endpoints.
May be skipped during ordinary development because it probes the configured Base Sepolia RPC.
May be skipped during ordinary development because it probes the deployed testnet indexer and compares it to RPC head.
May be skipped during ordinary development because it probes public deployed app URLs.
May be skipped during ordinary development because it fetches deployed app assets to inspect runtime config.
May be skipped during ordinary development because it probes deployed testnet contract bytecode through RPC.
May be skipped during ordinary development because the intended check mutates deployed testnet state with a verifier-funded transaction.
May be skipped during ordinary development because browser journeys against the deployed site are slower and may eventually drive wallet/testnet state.
